statement.p: You are ready to self-publish a book online. If the book were free, you estimate 50000 would order a copy. If you increase the price to p dollars per book you will have fewer orders, and after studying similar book sales you estimate you would have {50000-1000p} sales. What is the largest price that you could set to end up with a revenue of {\$40{,}000}\text{?}

solution.p: If you set the price at p dollars per book, your revenue will be (\text{number sold})\cdot\text{price}\text{,} which is ({50000-1000p})p\text{,} which simplifies to {-1000p^{2}+50000p}\text{.} We need to solve the equation {-1000p^{2}+50000p}=40000\text{.} In standard form, this is {-1000p^{2}+50000p-40000}=0\text{,} and the quadratic formula says:

The possible solutions are {49.1868} and {0.813227}\text{.} They are both valid solutions, but the question was to find the largest price we could set to reach a profit of \text{.} So we should set the price to be {\$49.19}\text{.}
